title: For a Door Opening
scripture_text: Behold, I am doing a new thing; can you not perceive it? I will make a way in the wilderness and rivers in the wasteland.
reflection: A new thing is rarely comfortable. The new thing asks you to leave the old thing, and the old thing is at least familiar.
body:
The call came. The offer came. The opportunity you had stopped praying for showed up in your inbox. You are excited. You are terrified. Both are correct.

The Lord is doing a new thing. He has been doing new things since the beginning of time. Each one required someone to step out of a boat, leave a country, walk into a furnace, give up a meal ticket, follow a stranger.

You are the next one in the line. Step. The fear is not a sign you are doing it wrong. The fear is a sign you are doing it right.

The Lord, who called you to it, will be faithful in it.
prayer: Lord, I am stepping. Hold my hand. Hold my heart. And do not let me look back. Amen.
